data.title: Build-A-Die
data.solution:
from itertools import permutations


class CubeFace:
    def __init__(self, adj: tuple[str, str, str, str]):
        self._adj = "".join(adj)

    def check(self, left: str, right: str = "") -> bool:
        return left + right in self._adj + self._adj[0]


class CubeNet:
    def __init__(self, net: str):
        self._net = net
        self._map = self._parse_net(net)

    def _parse_net(self, net: str) -> dict[tuple[int, int], str]:
        """Cube net 1/11:
        ???         ?
         ?   ?      ?      ?
         ?   ????   ?   ????
         ?   ?     ???     ?
        """
        if net == "???\n ? \n ? \n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (0, 1): "front",
                (0, 2): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "left",
                (2, 1): "back",
                (3, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "?   \n????\n?   ":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 0): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == "   ?\n????\n   ?":
            return {
                (0, 3): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 3): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == " ? \n ? \n ? \n???":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 1): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 1): "back",
                (3, 0): "left",
                (3, 2): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 2/11:
        ??          ?
         ??   ?     ?      ?
         ?   ????  ??   ????
         ?   ?      ??    ?
        """
        if net == "?? \n ??\n ? \n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (0, 1): "front",
                (1, 2): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "left",
                (2, 1): "back",
                (3, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == " ?  \n????\n?   ":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 0): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == "   ?\n????\n  ? ":
            return {
                (0, 3): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 2): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == " ? \n ? \n?? \n ??":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 1): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 1): "back",
                (2, 0): "left",
                (3, 2): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 3/11:
        ??          ?
         ?     ?   ??      ?
         ??  ????   ?   ????
         ?   ?      ??   ?
        """
        if net == "?? \n ? \n ??\n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (0, 1): "front",
                (2, 2): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "left",
                (2, 1): "back",
                (3, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "  ? \n????\n?   ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 0): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == "   ?\n????\n ?  ":
            return {
                (0, 3): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == " ? \n?? \n ? \n ??":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 2): "back",
                (3, 1): "left",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 4/11:
        ??
         ?      ?
         ?   ????
         ??  ?
        """
        if net == "?? \n ? \n ? \n ??":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (0, 1): "front",
                (3, 2): "bottom",
                (2, 1): "back",
                (1, 1): "left",
                (3, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "   ?\n????\n?   ":
            return {
                (0, 3): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 0): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }

        """Cube net 5/11:
        ?           ?
        ???   ?     ?     ??
         ?    ???  ???  ???
         ?   ??      ?    ?
        """
        if net == "?  \n???\n ? \n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 1): "left",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == " ?  \n ???\n??  ":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 1): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (2, 0): "left",
                (1, 3): "back",
                (1, 2): "right",
            }
        if net == "  ??\n??? \n  ? ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (0, 3): "front",
                (2, 2): "bottom",
                (1, 2): "left",
                (1, 1): "back",
                (1, 0): "right",
            }
        if net == " ? \n ? \n???\n  ?":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 1): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (2, 0): "left",
                (3, 2): "back",
                (2, 2): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 6/11:
        ?           ?
        ??     ?   ??     ??
         ??   ???   ??  ???
         ?   ??      ?   ?
        """
        if net == "?  \n?? \n ??\n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 1): "left",
                (2, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "  ? \n ???\n??  ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (1, 1): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (2, 0): "left",
                (1, 3): "back",
                (1, 2): "right",
            }
        if net == "  ??\n??? \n ?  ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (0, 3): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (1, 2): "left",
                (1, 1): "back",
                (1, 0): "right",
            }
        if net == " ? \n?? \n ??\n  ?":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 2): "left",
                (2, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 7/11:
        ?          ??
        ??      ?   ?     ??
         ?    ???   ??  ???
         ??  ??      ?  ?
        """
        if net == "?  \n?? \n ? \n ??":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 1): "left",
                (3, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "   ?\n ???\n??  ":
            return {
                (0, 3): "top",
                (1, 1): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (1, 2): "right",
                (1, 3): "back",
                (2, 0): "left",
            }
        if net == "  ??\n??? \n?   ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (0, 3): "front",
                (2, 0): "bottom",
                (1, 0): "right",
                (1, 1): "back",
                (1, 2): "left",
            }
        if net == "?? \n ? \n ??\n  ?":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (0, 1): "front",
                (2, 2): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "left",
                (2, 1): "back",
                (3, 2): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 8/11:
        ?
        ??     ??
         ??   ??
          ?  ??
        """
        if net == "?  \n?? \n ??\n  ?":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 2): "left",
                (2, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "  ??\n ?? \n??  ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (0, 3): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (1, 2): "left",
                (1, 1): "back",
                (2, 0): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 9/11:
         ?          ?
        ???   ?     ?     ?
         ?   ????  ???  ????
         ?    ?     ?     ?
        """
        if net == " ? \n???\n ? \n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 1): "left",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == " ?  \n????\n ?  ":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == "  ? \n????\n  ? ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 2): "bottom",
                (1, 1): "right",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 3): "left",
            }
        if net == " ? \n ? \n???\n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 1): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (2, 0): "left",
                (3, 1): "back",
                (2, 2): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 10/11:
         ?
        ??     ?
         ??  ????
         ?    ?
        """
        if net == " ? \n?? \n ??\n ? ":
            return {
                (0, 1): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (3, 1): "left",
                (2, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "  ? \n????\n ?  ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 1): "bottom",
                (1, 3): "left",
                (1, 2): "back",
                (1, 1): "right",
            }

        """Cube net 11/11:
        ?
        ?
        ??
         ?    ???
         ?  ???
        """
        if net == "? \n? \n??\n ?\n ?":
            return {
                (0, 0): "top",
                (1, 0): "front",
                (2, 0): "bottom",
                (4, 1): "left",
                (3, 1): "back",
                (2, 1): "right",
            }
        if net == "  ???\n???  ":
            return {
                (0, 2): "top",
                (0, 3): "front",
                (0, 4): "bottom",
                (1, 2): "left",
                (1, 1): "back",
                (1, 0): "right",
            }

        assert False, "Invalid cube net"

    def _check(self, faces: tuple[str, ...], constraints: list[str]) -> bool:
        top, front, bottom, back, left, right = faces
        cube_faces = {
            top: CubeFace((front, right, back, left)),
            front: CubeFace((top, left, bottom, right)),
            bottom: CubeFace((front, left, back, right)),
            back: CubeFace((top, right, bottom, left)),
            left: CubeFace((top, back, bottom, front)),
            right: CubeFace((top, front, bottom, back)),
        }

        for constraint in constraints:
            if len(constraint) == 2:
                a, b = constraint[0], constraint[1]
                if not (cube_faces[a].check(b) and cube_faces[b].check(a)):
                    return False
            elif len(constraint) == 3:
                a, b, c = constraint[0], constraint[1], constraint[2]
                if not (
                    cube_faces[a].check(b, c)
                    and cube_faces[b].check(c, a)
                    and cube_faces[c].check(a, b)
                ):
                    return False
            else:
                assert False, "Invalid constraint"
        return True

    def assign(self, faces: str, constraints: list[str]) -> list[str]:
        res: dict[str, str] = {}
        seen: set[str] = set()

        for perm in permutations(faces):
            # Rotate cube such that the first (top) face is the smallest letter
            # and the second (front) face is the smallest letter among the remaining faces
            idx = min(range(6), key=lambda i: perm[i])
            if idx == 0:
                sides = (perm[1] + perm[5] + perm[3] + perm[4]) * 2
                min_sides = min(
                    sides[i : i + 4] for i in range(4)
                )  # front, right, back, left
                rotated_perm = (
                    perm[0]
                    + min_sides[0]
                    + perm[2]
                    + min_sides[2]
                    + min_sides[3]
                    + min_sides[1]
                )
            elif idx == 1:
                sides = (perm[2] + perm[5] + perm[0] + perm[4]) * 2
                min_sides = min(
                    sides[i : i + 4] for i in range(4)
                )  # front, right, back, left
                rotated_perm = (
                    perm[1]
                    + min_sides[0]
                    + perm[3]
                    + min_sides[2]
                    + min_sides[3]
                    + min_sides[1]
                )
            elif idx == 2:
                sides = (perm[3] + perm[5] + perm[1] + perm[4]) * 2
                min_sides = min(
                    sides[i : i + 4] for i in range(4)
                )  # front, right, back, left
                rotated_perm = (
                    perm[2]
                    + min_sides[0]
                    + perm[0]
                    + min_sides[2]
                    + min_sides[3]
                    + min_sides[1]
                )
            elif idx == 3:
                sides = (perm[0] + perm[5] + perm[2] + perm[4]) * 2
                min_sides = min(
                    sides[i : i + 4] for i in range(4)
                )  # front, right, back, left
                rotated_perm = (
                    perm[3]
                    + min_sides[0]
                    + perm[1]
                    + min_sides[2]
                    + min_sides[3]
                    + min_sides[1]
                )
            elif idx == 4:
                sides = (perm[1] + perm[0] + perm[3] + perm[2]) * 2
                min_sides = min(
                    sides[i : i + 4] for i in range(4)
                )  # front, right, back, left
                rotated_perm = (
                    perm[4]
                    + min_sides[0]
                    + perm[5]
                    + min_sides[2]
                    + min_sides[3]
                    + min_sides[1]
                )
            else:
                sides = (perm[1] + perm[2] + perm[3] + perm[0]) * 2
                min_sides = min(
                    sides[i : i + 4] for i in range(4)
                )  # front, right, back, left
                rotated_perm = (
                    perm[5]
                    + min_sides[0]
                    + perm[4]
                    + min_sides[2]
                    + min_sides[3]
                    + min_sides[1]
                )

            # Determine if rotation is valid and if it has been seen before
            if rotated_perm not in seen:
                seen.add(rotated_perm)
                if not self._check(tuple(rotated_perm), constraints):
                    continue
            elif rotated_perm not in res:
                continue

            # Map the faces to their corresponding letters and construct the net
            perm_map = {
                face: letter
                for face, letter in zip(
                    ("top", "front", "bottom", "back", "left", "right"), perm
                )
            }
            net = [list(row) for row in self._net.split("\n")]
            for (i, j), face in self._map.items():
                net[i][j] = perm_map[face]
            net_flat = "\n".join("".join(row) for row in net)
            if rotated_perm not in res or net_flat < res[rotated_perm]:
                res[rotated_perm] = net_flat

        return list(sorted(res.values()))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    faces = input()

    N = int(input())
    constraints_grid = [input() for _ in range(5)]
    constraints: list[str] = []
    for j in range(0, 6 * N, 6):
        if constraints_grid[0][j] == "\\":
            constraints.append(
                constraints_grid[0][j + 2]
                + constraints_grid[3][j]
                + constraints_grid[3][j + 4]
            )
        elif constraints_grid[0][j] == " ":
            constraints.append(constraints_grid[1][j + 2] + constraints_grid[3][j + 2])
        elif constraints_grid[0][j] == "-":
            constraints.append(constraints_grid[2][j] + constraints_grid[2][j + 4])
        else:
            assert False, "Invalid constraint grid"

    W, H = map(int, input().split())
    net = "\n".join(input() for _ in range(H))
    cube_net = CubeNet(net)

    valid_nets = cube_net.assign(faces, constraints)
    print(len(valid_nets))
    for net in sorted(valid_nets):
        for row in net.split("\n"):
            print(row.rstrip())
data.statement:
Thank goodness you're here, toymaker. The factory's toy production has been stumped lately by a termite infestation that has chewed away portions of our die blueprints! We need your help to decipher whatever's left so that production can resume.

A die is a standard cube with {{6}} labelled faces. For each die blueprint, you will be given the labels of all {{6}} faces and some 3D images of the fully constructed die that show some of the faces of the fully constructed die. Based on this information, please reconstruct the provided die template by labelling each face on the template!

data.constraints:
0 ≤ [[N]] ≤ 4
2 ≤ [[W]], [[H]] ≤ 5
0 ≤ [[T]] ≤ 30
Each face label is a printable non-whitespace non-question-mark ASCII character
Each given die template is a valid cube net with exactly {{6}} question marks ({{?}})
data.stubGenerator:
read faces:word(6)
read n:int
loop 5 read image:string(23)
read w:int h:int
loop h read die:string(w)
write die templates!
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1>>: {{6}} face labels used on the die, in no particular order
<<Line 2>>: A single non-negative integer [[N]]; the number of 3D images of the fully constructed die
<<Next {{5}} lines>>: [[N]] space-separated sets of {{5}}x{{5}} 3D images showing the fully constructed die viewed from a certain angle. Each of the [[N]] images will either show 2 or 3 faces in one of the following 3 ways:
```  (1)  |  (2)  |  (3)
 \ A / |       | --+--
  \ /  | / A \ |   |  
   v   | +---+ | B | C
 B | C | | B | |   |  
   |   |       | --+--```Note: labels in each image are <<not guaranteed>> to be properly oriented. If [[N]] = 0, these 5 lines will be empty.
<<Next line>>: Two space-separated positive integers [[W]] and [[H]]
<<Next [[H]] lines>>: Space-padded lines of length [[W]] denoting the die template as a cube net. Each question mark ({{?}}) represents an unlabelled face on the cube net
data.outputDescription:
<<Line 1>>: A single non-negative integer [[T]]; the number of <<unique>> die template(s) constructible from the given die blueprint
<<Next [[T]]x[[H]] lines>>: [[T]] sets of [[H]] lines, where each set denotes a valid die template with its faces labelled with the {{6}} given face labels - one label per face

- Two die templates are <<equal>> if their folded dice can be rotated such that every pair of corresponding faces match.
- Each unique die template must be the <<lexicographically smallest>> among its valid permutations. Output the valid die template(s) in <<ascending lexicographical order>>.
- Remove trailing spaces per output line.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

Thank goodness you're here, toymaker. The factory's toy production has been stumped lately by a termite infestation that has chewed away portions of our die blueprints! We need your help to decipher whatever's left so that production can resume.

A die is a standard cube with 6 labelled faces. For each die blueprint, you will be given the labels of all 6 faces and some 3D images of the fully constructed die that show some of the faces of the fully constructed die. Based on this information, please reconstruct the provided die template by labelling each face on the template!
Input
Line 1: 6 face labels used on the die, in no particular order
Line 2: A single non-negative integer N; the number of 3D images of the fully constructed die
Next 5 lines: N space-separated sets of 5x5 3D images showing the fully constructed die viewed from a certain angle. Each of the N images will either show 2 or 3 faces in one of the following 3 ways:
  (1)  |  (2)  |  (3)
 \ A / |       | --+--
  \ /  | / A \ |   |  
   v   | +---+ | B | C
 B | C | | B | |   |  
   |   |       | --+--
Note: labels in each image are not guaranteed to be properly oriented. If N = 0, these 5 lines will be empty.
Next line: Two space-separated positive integers W and H
Next H lines: Space-padded lines of length W denoting the die template as a cube net. Each question mark (?) represents an unlabelled face on the cube net

   

   

      
Output

      
Line 1: A single non-negative integer T; the number of unique die template(s) constructible from the given die blueprint
Next TxH lines: T sets of H lines, where each set denotes a valid die template with its faces labelled with the 6 given face labels - one label per face

- Two die templates are equal if their folded dice can be rotated such that every pair of corresponding faces match.
- Each unique die template must be the lexicographically smallest among its valid permutations. Output the valid die template(s) in ascending lexicographical order.
- Remove trailing spaces per output line.

   

   

      
Constraints

      
0 ≤ N ≤ 4
2 ≤ W, H ≤ 5
0 ≤ T ≤ 30
Each face label is a printable non-whitespace non-question-mark ASCII character
Each given die template is a valid cube net with exactly 6 question marks (?)

   

   

      
Example

      

         

            
Input

            
ABCDEF
3
\ F / \ E / \ D /
 \ /   \ /   \ / 
  v     v     v  
A | D C | F A | B
  |     |     |  
3 4
???
 ? 
 ? 
 ? 

         

         

            
Output

            
1
ABE
 C
 F
 D
